title: 10 best herbal teas for oily hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily hair are infusions made from various herbs, which can help regulate sebum production and improve the overall condition of oily hair. These teas often contain natural astringents and anti-inflammatory properties that can gently cleanse and soothe the scalp, reducing excess oiliness. Some examples of herbal teas beneficial for oily hair include green tea, chamomile, sage, and nettle. By incorporating these herbal teas into hair care routines, individuals can experience a reduction in greasiness, improved scalp health, and increased hair volume, leading to a noticeable improvement in their confidence and self-esteem.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The tea's antifungal and antibacterial compounds also work to eliminate dirt, sweat, and product buildup that can weigh down the hair and exacerbate oiliness. By drinking lemon balm tea, individuals can promote a healthy scalp environment that reduces excess sebum secretion, leading to cleaner, healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking associated with oily hair.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in lemon balm tea help to tighten pores and reduce oil production on the scalp, leaving hair feeling cleaner and less greasy.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, reducing redness and discomfort caused by oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves help to balance oil production in the scalp, reducing excess oil and promoting a healthy shine.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves for every 8 oz cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried lemon balm leaves in the boiling water for 7-10 minutes to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Combine the strained tea with honey and/or lemon juice to taste, adjusting sweetness as needed.
Serve the herbal lemon balm tea hot or chilled within 24 hours of preparation for optimal flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with oily hair because it is rich in silica, a natural mineral that can help to balance the scalp's oil production. The silica in horsetail tea works to strengthen the hair follicles, reducing the amount of oil that is produced and absorbed into the hair shaft. Additionally, horsetail tea has antioxidant properties that help to remove impurities and excess oils from the hair, leaving it looking cleaner and healthier. Regular consumption of horsetail tea can lead to a noticeable reduction in oily locks.
remedy_properties: Sodium Silicate: Horsetail tea contains high levels of sodium silicate, which helps to absorb excess oil from the scalp and hair, leaving them feeling cleaner and more manageable.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in horsetail tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oily hair by breaking down the scalp's natural barrier function.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, leading to a reduction in oil production and an improvement in overall hair health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, roots help balance oil production and strengthen hair follicles.
Stems: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, stems reduce dandruff and flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried horsetail root per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a medium saucepan and bring it to a rolling boil for 5-7 minutes.
Add the measured horsetail root to the boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large pitcher.
Cool the tea to room temperature and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The tea's active compounds, such as citric acid and limonene, work to cleanse the pores and remove excess sebum, leaving the hair looking healthier and less greasy. Regular use of large-leaved lime teas can also help to reduce dandruff and itchiness, further promoting a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Large-leaved lime tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in large-leaved lime tea soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, which can be caused by excessive oil production and irritation.
Astringent properties: Large-leaved lime tea's astringent properties help to dry out the scalp and hair follicles, reducing excess oil production and leaving the hair looking cleaner and healthier.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the large-leaved lime plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to help balance excess oil production and reduce dandruff, making them a great addition to teas for oily hair.
Bark: The bark of the large-leaved lime plant is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia platyphyllos leaves in quantities of 20-30 grams for every liter of tea.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold filtered water to remove any impurities and debris gently.
Combine the cleaned leaves with one liter of boiling water in a large saucepan and let it steep for 5-7 minutes carefully.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids quickly.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature before refrigerating or serving, allowing flavors to mature slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with oily hair because it contains antioxidants and antiseptic properties that help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing excess oil production. The tea's astringent qualities also help to constrict pores, minimizing sebum buildup on the surface of the skin. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties work to combat fungal and bacterial overgrowth, common contributors to oily hair, promoting a healthier scalp and more manageable locks.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the amount of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness and dandruff.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in thyme tea help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing excess sebum production and absorption, leading to a healthier and less oily hair condition.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the hair follicles and lead to oil buildup, promoting healthy hair growth and balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat scalp issues, reducing oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
Bark: The bark of the thyme plant contains astringent compounds that help constrict pores, reducing excess sebum production and controlling oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves in sufficient quantity for desired steeping strength.
Rinse harvested thymus vulgaris leaves with filtered water to remove impurities and debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors and active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and enjoy warm or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with oily hair because it stimulates blood flow to the scalp, which can help to balance the production of sebum. The antioxidants and essential oils present in rosemary tea also work to purify the scalp and remove impurities that can contribute to oiliness. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to tighten the cuticle layer of the hair shaft, reducing frizz and adding shine to oily locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Rosemary tea's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and lead to excessive oil production, reducing the appearance of greasiness.
Stimulants: The stimulant compounds in rosemary tea increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps to reduce excess oil production and promote healthy hair growth.
Astringents: Rosemary tea's astringent properties help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing sebum secretion and minimizing the appearance of oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help control sebum production and reduce scalp irritation.
Leaves: High in essential oils, leaves provide natural astringency to tighten pores, reduce oiliness, and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves from the rosmarinus officinalis plant.
Measure one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves or two teaspoons of fresh rosemary leaves.
Steep the rosemary leaves in one cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper.
Add honey or lemon to taste and serve hot, allowing it to cool if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram teas helps with oily hair because its natural antiseptic and astringent properties help to control sebum production, reducing excess oil in the scalp. The tea's ability to balance pH levels also helps to regulate the hair follicles, preventing clogged pores and dandruff that can contribute to oily hair. As a result, marjoram teas promote healthy, balanced hair growth while minimizing the appearance of greasiness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Marjoram tea's antioxidants help to combat free radicals that can cause damage to the scalp and hair, leading to dryness and irritation.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in marjoram tea help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing excess oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm an itchy, irritated scalp, reducing redness and flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Marjoram leaves help to regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil and balancing the scalp's natural moisture.
Roots: Marjoram roots have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marjoram leaves or use dried marjoram flowers according to your preference.
Measure one teaspoon of dried marjoram or three sprigs of fresh marjoram for every 8 ounces of water needed.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle to a rolling boil then let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids using a fine-mesh sieve or tea infuser immediately after brewing.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation for best flavor and aroma preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with oily hair because its antiseptic and astringent properties help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. When consumed as a tea, sage's essential oils are absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the scalp, where they work to reduce excess sebum secretion. This results in a more balanced moisture level, reducing the appearance of greasiness and leaving hair feeling cleaner and healthier for longer. Additionally, sage's natural antibacterial properties help to combat dandruff and other scalp irritations that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Sage tea's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat scalp irritations, infections, and fungi that can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in sage tea help to dry up excess oil on the scalp and hair, reducing grease and flakiness while balancing the natural pH of the scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Sage tea's antioxidants help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used to create teas that help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and promoting a balanced scalp.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant contains flavonoids that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves, measuring one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the harvested sage leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, allowing it to steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or tea infuser, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Add honey or lemon to taste, stirring well to dissolve any sweetness or citrus flavorings.
Enjoy the warm herbal tea immediately, taking at least two cups daily for optimal health benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with oily hair because its antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to balance the scalp's natural pH levels, reducing excess oil production. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties tighten the pores on the scalp, minimizing oil leakage and leaving the hair looking cleaner and more manageable. As an added benefit, the calming effects of lavender can also soothe an irritated scalp, leading to healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lavender tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat the bacteria on the scalp that can contribute to oil production and dandruff, promoting a healthier scalp environment.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in lavender tea help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to oily hair, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm an itchy, irritated scalp, reducing redness and flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots can help balance the scalp's natural oil production and reduce dandruff.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, bark has astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce excess oil secretion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried english lavender flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured lavender flowers in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the fragrant oils and flavors from the lavender.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer to capture the loose herbs.
Serve the herbal tea hot, garnished with a sprig of fresh lavender if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with oily hair because it contains thujone, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce sebum production in the scalp. This results in a decrease in oiliness, leaving hair feeling lighter and less greasy. Additionally, wormwood's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to eliminate dirt and product buildup, further reducing the appearance of oily roots.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Wormwood tea helps to reduce excess oil production by constricting the pores on the scalp, thereby reducing sebum buildup and greasiness.
Antibacterial properties: Wormwood's antibacterial properties help to combat scalp infections and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe an itchy and irritated scalp, reducing redness and flaking that can be associated with oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are often used to make teas that help balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing dandruff and flaking.
Seeds: The seeds of the wormwood plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe oily scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh wormwood leaves or purchase dried artemisia absinthium.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried wormwood or 3-4 sprigs of fresh wormwood into infuser.
Combine wormwood with boiling water in a teapot or mug for 5-7 minutes steeping.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or pitcher.
Enjoy the brewed wormwood tea hot or allow it to cool before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch teas helps with oily hair because it contains alpha-hydroxy acids, which help to break down sebum and balance the scalp's natural pH. This can reduce excess oil production, leaving hair feeling cleaner and lighter. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe an irritated scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness. As a result, silver birch teas can help to regulate oily hair, promoting a healthier and more manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Astringent and antiseptic properties: Silver birch tea's astringent and antiseptic properties help to reduce excess oil production by shrinking pores and killing bacteria that contribute to oil buildup on the scalp.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of silver birch tea can help to calm irritated scalps, reduce redness and itching associated with oily hair, and promote healthy blood circulation to the scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Silver birch tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair and scalp problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help to purify the scalp and reduce oil production.
Bark: Containing salicin, a natural beta-lipid, the bark helps to regulate sebum production and soothe irritated scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silver birch leaves from sustainable sources.
Combine one teaspoon of crushed silver birch leaves with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven minutes before straining and cooling.
Store the cooled tea in airtight containers to preserve freshness.
Consume two cups of tea daily for a period of two weeks.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with cooled tea made from chamomile, sage, or nettle. These herbs have astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness. For optimal results, use twice a week after shampooing. Avoid over-brewing the tea, as this can lead to dryness and counteract the desired effect. Remember, individual results may vary, and it's essential to maintain a balanced diet and healthy lifestyle for overall hair health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and an itchy scalp. Herbal teas like chamomile, nettle, and sage have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp and reduce dandruff. These teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or can be combined with other ingredients to create a hair mask. Additionally, green tea, which is rich in antioxidants, can help regulate oil production and promote hair growth. Regular use of these herbal teas can lead to a healthier scalp and shinier, more manageable hair.
